problem_statement: Admin.tsx is an oversized monolithic component that bundles multiple responsibilities (data display, CRUD operations, configuration management) into a single file. This makes the component difficult to reason about, test in isolation, and modify safely. Decomposition into focused modules improves readability, testability, and maintenance.
in_scope: Analyse Admin.tsx to identify distinct responsibilities and logical boundaries
Split Admin.tsx into focused child components and/or utility modules
Use 200 LOC as a preferred target for resulting modules but do not force artificial splits where readability would worsen
Ensure the decomposed components compose to produce identical behaviour to the original Admin.tsx
Apply the typed prop interfaces defined in P2-R2-06 to the new child components
out_of_scope: Adding new admin features or capabilities — R2 is purely structural
Visual or design changes to the admin UI — styling remains as-is
Decomposing other components — each hotspot has its own task
Changing the data model or API endpoints used by Admin
requirements: Admin.tsx is decomposed into focused modules with clear single responsibilities
Resulting modules target approximately 200 LOC each unless a larger module preserves better readability
All child components have typed prop interfaces
The composed result produces identical behaviour and rendering to the pre-decomposition Admin.tsx
acceptance_criteria: Admin.tsx is decomposed into clearer modules with preserved behaviour
No new features, visual changes, or API changes are introduced
npm run check passes with no new type errors introduced
The admin section functions identically to the pre-refactor version
dependencies: P2-R2-06 — typed prop interfaces for Admin and its children should be defined
P3-R2-08 — shared hooks (useStages, useSubStages) should be available if Admin uses them
P3-R2-09 — apiRequest wrapper should be available if Admin makes API calls
risks: Tightly coupled internal state may make clean decomposition difficult; may need to lift state or use context
implementation_notes: Identify natural boundaries: data tables, forms, action buttons, filters. Extract each into its own component file in an Admin/ directory or similar. Use the shared hooks from P3-R2-08 for data fetching. Pass data and callbacks via typed props.
